title: Vialco

{{Quote|No, no more dreams, girl. I've come for the harvest. What shall it be, hmm?|Vialco|<ref name="uek" />}}
'''Vialco''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] in the service of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. An apprentice of [[High Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]], he forged a reputation for [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] down fugitive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Mind trick/Legends#Force illusion|pervading]] their [[Dream/Legends|dreams]] with disturbing visions of his own design. He served with the [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] on [[Nysza III]], where the Imperials were constructing a new [[communications bunker]]. At some stage, the construction was stymied by the arrival of the [[Harrier Infiltration Team]], agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Vialco [[Battle of Nysza III|defended]] the construction site, [[Death/Legends|slaying]] the [[Agent/Legends|agent]] [[Arecelis Acosta]], and battling the aspirant Jedi [[Fable Astin/Legends|Fable Astin]].

Vialco overcame the Jedi, who along with [[Deke Holman]] was the only member of the infiltration team to escape. The Dark Jedi hunted Astin, and through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] plagued her dreams with nightmarish visions of her doom, in which he would appear to frighten her. He eventually tracked her to [[Trulalis]], where she had been training under the auspices of former [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Adalric Cessius Brandl]]. Vialco [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueled]] the young Jedi, and soon discovered that her skills had been greatly enhanced by Brandl. The [[Duel on Trulalis|duel]] came to an end when Vialco was [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|outfenced]] by Astin, and when he was at her mercy, the Jedi unceremoniously [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitated]] him.

==Biography==
===Defender of Nysza III===
{{Quote|He's coming for me.|Fable Astin|<ref name="uek" />}}
[[File:Fable_Astin2_SWJ8.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Fable Astin, Vialco's adversary, mourns the loss of Arecelis Acosta at the Dark Jedi's hands.]]
Vialco, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]]. A [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], he trained under the auspices of the [[High Inquisitor]] and former [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi Padawan]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]], and assumed that he would ultimately inherit his master's title in due course.<ref name="sidebar" /> He was known to individuals such as the rogue [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Adalric Cessius Brandl]], having gained infamy for his [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic abilities]]. Vialco would prey on the weak-minded, [[Mind trick/Legends#Force illusion|inserting visions and illusions]] in their minds—in Brandl's opinion, however, he was a coward.<ref name="uek" /> During his service to Tremayne, Vialco was diligent in stalking and [[Death/Legends|killing]] any fugitive [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] that he could, and managed to avoid punishment from both his master and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] despite finding it difficult to serve under non-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] commanding [[Officer/Legends|officers]].<ref name="sidebar" />

In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="erc" /> Vialco was assigned to the Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] on [[Nysza III]], where the Empire was constructing a new [[communications bunker]]. In due course, [[Agent/Legends|agents]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]—the [[Harrier Infiltration Team]]—attacked the construction site. The team, consisting of [[Socorran]] [[Deke Holman]], [[Coynite/Legends|Coynite]]/Human [[Hybrid/Legends|half-breed]] [[Arecelis Acosta]], and aspiring [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Fable Astin/Legends|Fable Astin]] were able to enter the site, with the intent of destroying the installment. Vialco [[Battle of Nysza III|confronted]] Acosta and toyed with him before finally [[Death/Legends|slaying]] the half-breed. He then moved against Astin directly, and a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] commenced in one of the construction corridors. With a feint and a defensive maneuver, Vialco was able to send Astin flying across the garrison. As the Dark Jedi mocked her, she gave into the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and lashed out in anger. Astin and Holman were then able to destroy the construction site and escape Nysza III.<ref name="uek" />

Vialco survived, however, and through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], reached out to Astin, pervading her dreams. It was his hope that he would be able to turn Astin fully to the dark side. In his first effort, her mind was cast back to her mission, before she had encountered Vialco and was trapped by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], along with Acosta. In the dream, Vialco took Acosta's place and attacked Astin, luring her with the power of the dark side. The Jedi woke from her dream, but Vialco had been able to leave no doubt in her mind that he was coming for her, and that he meant for her to embrace the dark side. Vialco reached out to Astin a second [[Time/Legends|time]], when she was flying an [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. He conjured a vision of Acosta's body and sent it flying into the [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter's]] [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]], through the windshield. To the horrified Astin, it felt as though the air were being sucked from her [[Lung/Legends|lungs]] as her cockpit was now open to [[Realspace/Legends|space]], and Vialco appeared to her again, mockingly laughing at her. Vialco's vision then ceased, leaving Astin perturbed.<ref name="uek" />

===Death on Trulalis===
{{Quote|Vialco's undoing is inevitable. Even I have seen this.|Adalric Brandl|<ref name="uek" />}}
[[File:Fable_Astin_vs_Vialco_SWJ8.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Vialco duels Fable Astin on the plains of Trulalis.]]
It came to Vialco's attention that Astin was in training on [[Trulalis]], under the instruction of none other than Inquisitor Brandl. Journeying to Trulalis in a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], he landed near the [[City/Legends|township]] of [[Kovit]]. Disembarking, the Dark Jedi made his way to [[The Barrows (Trulalis)|The Barrows]], a graveyard, and there he waited for Astin. The Jedi soon approached, and Vialco was surprised at her newfound maturity. In particular, he was surprised that the infamously vindictive Brandl had even taken her in. He reached out and [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] Astin's thoughts—she was assuring herself that she would experience no more bad dreams. Announcing his intent to kill her, Vialco drew his weapon and [[Duel on Trulalis|attacked]], but was once again surprised when she successfully parried his strikes without sacrificing any ground.<ref name="uek" />

The two bandied words, and Vialco doubled his attack, his anger rising. Conjuring another illusion in her mind, he brought back the memory of Acosta, but Astin was not to be fooled. She sliced at Vialco's shoulder and sent him hurtling against the tomb of [[Adalric Brandl's wife|Brandl's wife]]. Astin taunted Vialco, threatening to do likewise to him as he had done to Acosta. Enraged, Vialco realized that he could not convert Astin to the dark side, and summoned [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], hurling it at his opponent. He struck her with three bolts, bringing her to the ground. As he gloated, she rose to her feet once more, and he continued his barrage. This time, however, Astin was able to deflect the bolts with her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], anticipating each of Vialco's attacks.<ref name="uek" />

She gradually made her way toward him and knocked the Dark Jedi into a depression. The barrage of lightning began to turn against Vialco, burning him severely. He reached out for his fallen weapon, but fumbled, only causing the weapon to move further from him. Astin approached, weapon held aloft with the intent to kill. The Dark Jedi writhed, adopting a grovelling manner, and the Jedi hesitated. Lord Brandl approached, and insisted that Astin kill Vialco, lest he invade her dreams once more. Although Astin hesitated briefly, she lashed out and [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitated]] the Dark Jedi swiftly. Brandl had inserted an illusion of his own into her mind, of Vialco rising up with his weapon drawn, inspiring Astin's decisive action. It had been Brandl's intent to turn Astin to the dark side himself and present her to [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] on [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. The young Jedi, however, fled with the assistance of Brandl's son, [[Jaalib Brandl|Jaalib]], and her compatriot Holman.<ref name="uek" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Have we come so far to fall so low? Tsk, tsk, what a pity.|Vialco|<ref name="uek" />}}
Vialco was considered an unattractive man, with an angular face, gaunt cheeks, [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and unusually large eyebrows. When haunting Fable Astin, Vialco became overly vindictive, reminding her of her failure to prevent Arecelis Acosta from being killed. He used the incident in an attempt to sway Astin to the dark side, stating that had she given in to her passion, she would have been able to save Acosta. It was Vialco's belief that anger provided control over actions, that power could stem from fear itself. He referred to the dark side as the "ultimate ecstasy," and held the belief that it was the true form of power. When truly threatened, however, Vialco would resort to cowardly behavior, grovelling before Astin when she had bested him in combat.<ref name="uek" /> The Inquisitor was an ambitious man, seeking to further his position within the Empire and eventually take the position of his master, Antinnis Tremayne, as High Inquisitor, to such a point that Vialco assumed that his ascension would take place should the need arise. Vialco's success as a Jedi hunter came in spite of both his inability to work with non-Force sensitive officers and his own ego.<ref name="sidebar" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Vialco is a coward. His tactics are mere illusions, prey for the weak-minded.|Adalric Cessius Brandl|<ref name="uek" />}}
Vialco was a skilled [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combatant]] with a lightsaber, able to deftly outmach Fable Astin during their first encounter. His telepathic powers allowed him to prey on the weak-minded, and invade Astin's mind from great distances, affecting her very dreams. He could even insert himself into her thoughts, and goad her as she [[Sleep/Legends|slept]]. In a later conjured vision, Vialco was able to make it seem as though the air were being sucked from Astin's lungs and into the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] of space, although in reality she was perfectly safe. Vialco was also able to do this in the heat of combat. The Dark Jedi could summon powerful bursts of Force lightning, but when he was knocked off his feet, he could no longer control his power, and the lightning turned on him, burning through his [[Clothing/Legends|apparel]] and flesh.<ref name="uek" /> Vialco could also use the Force to [[Tutaminis|absorb energy]], control pain and resist [[Stun/Legends|stun]] attacks, as well as detect the presence of the Force in others.<ref name="sidebar" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vialco was created by [[Patricia A. Jackson]], appearing in her [[1995]] short story, "[[Uhl Eharl Khoehng (short story)|Uhl Eharl Khoehng]]," which was published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]]. The character was later to be name-checked in Jackson's later work ''[[Emanations of Darkness]]'', but the story was ultimately canceled.
